title: portulaca constricta
intro: Portulaca constricta, also known as the caltrop or prickly purslane, is a species of flowering plant in the purslane family (Portulacaceae) native to the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Portulaca constricta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. The herbal preparations of Portulaca constricta typically involve infusions of the leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, salves, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
